title: Endocrine Embryology: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Endocrine Embryology
Identify key diagnostic features of Endocrine Embryology
Apply clinical reasoning to Endocrine Embryology management
Recognize common pitfalls in Endocrine Embryology

sections.title: Overview of Endocrine Embryology
sections.content:
**Endocrine Embryology**

Thyroid, parathyroid, adrenal, and pituitary gland development; pharyngeal pouch derivatives; common congenital anomalies

**Key Points:**
• Thyroid: foramen cecum → descends via thyroglossal duct → normal position at C5-T1
• Thyroglossal duct cyst: most common midline neck mass in children, moves with swallowing AND tongue protrusion
• Parathyroid: 3rd pouch (inferior) and 4th pouch (superior) — inferior parathyroids migrate FURTHER

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Thyroid: foramen cecum → descends via thyroglossal duct → normal position at C5-T1
• Thyroglossal duct cyst: most common midline neck mass in children, moves with swallowing AND tongue protrusion

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Parathyroid: 3rd pouch (inferior) and 4th pouch (superior) — inferior parathyroids migrate FURTHER
• Adrenal cortex: mesoderm; Adrenal medulla: neural crest cells
• Anterior pituitary: Rathke's pouch (oral ectoderm); Posterior pituitary: neuroectoderm (infundibulum)

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Endocrine Embryology:**

• Parathyroid: 3rd pouch (inferior) and 4th pouch (superior) — inferior parathyroids migrate FURTHER
• Adrenal cortex: mesoderm; Adrenal medulla: neural crest cells
• Anterior pituitary: Rathke's pouch (oral ectoderm); Posterior pituitary: neuroectoderm (infundibulum)

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Endocrine Embryology?

options.text: Thyroid: foramen cecum → descends via thyroglossal duct → normal position at C5-T1
options.explanation: Thyroid: foramen cecum → descends via thyroglossal duct → normal position at C5-T1

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for Endocrine Embryology?

options.text: Anterior pituitary: Rathke's pouch (oral ectoderm); Posterior pituitary: neuroectoderm (infundibulum)
options.explanation: Anterior pituitary: Rathke's pouch (oral ectoderm); Posterior pituitary: neuroectoderm (infundibulum)

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with Endocrine Embryology. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Thyroglossal duct cyst: most common midline neck mass in children, moves with swallowing AND tongue protrusion
options.explanation: Thyroglossal duct cyst: most common midline neck mass in children, moves with swallowing AND tongue protrusion
